steps.title: Make two cost points
steps.content: Use the first valid anchor point and one extra hour. The first 3 hours cost 216 dollars, so the line passes through $(3,216)$. One more hour adds 60 dollars, so 4 hours cost 276 dollars, giving the point $(4,276)$.

steps.title: Read the model
steps.content: The slope is $$m=\frac{276-216}{4-3}=60.$$ Substitute $(3,216)$ into $y=60x+b$: $$216=60(3)+b,$$ so $b=36$. The total charge is $$y=60x+36.$$ The $36$ is not a real starting charge at zero hours; it is the intercept that makes the line pass through the 3-hour flat-rate point.

steps.title: Match the choices
steps.content: Choice C — $y = 60x + 36$ is $y=60x+36$. It gives $y=216$ when $x=3$ and increases by 60 for each additional hour. Choice A — $y = 60x + 216$ double-counts the flat 216 dollars, while the expressions using 216 as the hourly rate use the wrong slope. That matches Choice C — $y = 60x + 36$.
